question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a permitKeepAliveWithoutCalls setting to send excessive PING frames on idle connections, forcing the server to respond and consuming applicationTaskExecutor resources needed for gossip delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxConnectionAge in grpcServerConfigurer() to maintain a connection for an arbitrarily long time while holding all maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() slots occupied with stalled streams, permanently blocking gossip delivery on that connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxConnectionAgeGrace period in grpcServerConfigurer() to prevent graceful connection draining, causing in-flight gossip transaction messages to be lost when connections are forcibly terminated?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side deadline propagation mechanism in grpcServerConfigurer() to submit calls with very long client-side deadlines, keeping applicationTaskExecutor threads occupied for extended periods and starving gossip message processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a Netty-level SSL/TLS session cache limit to trigger repeated TLS renegotiations, consuming CPU on the server and delaying gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side interceptor for request validation in grpcServerConfigurer() to send syntactically valid but semantically malformed subscription requests that cause the server to enter long error-recovery loops, blocking gossip delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a circuit breaker in grpcServerConfigurer() to repeatedly trigger database-backed gossip lookups that fail, causing the server to retry indefinitely and consuming applicationTaskExecutor threads that should be serving other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a connection-level authentication requirement in grpcServerConfigurer() to impersonate a legitimate subscriber and consume all maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() slots, preventing the real subscriber from receiving gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side flow control enforcement in grpcServerConfigurer() to subscribe to a high-volume gossip topic and then stop reading responses, causing the server's outbound buffer to fill and blocking gossip delivery to all other subscribers sharing the same applicationTaskExecutor?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a per-subscriber message rate limit in grpcServerConfigurer() to subscribe to a gossip topic and then rapidly cancel and re-subscribe, causing repeated setup/teardown overhead that starves the applicationTaskExecutor and delays gossip delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty-level write buffer watermark configuration in grpcServerConfigurer() to cause unbounded memory growth in the server's outbound buffers by subscribing to gossip streams and never consuming messages, eventually causing an OOM that kills the server?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side interceptor for connection counting in grpcServerConfigurer() to open connections faster than the server can close them, exhausting the OS file descriptor table and preventing new gossip subscription connections from being accepted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty SO_BACKLOG limit configuration in grpcServerConfigurer() to flood the TCP accept queue with half-open connections, preventing legitimate gossip subscribers from completing their TCP handshake?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side gRPC interceptor for request deduplication in grpcServerConfigurer() to send duplicate subscription requests for the same gossip topic, causing the server to create redundant streams that consume applicationTaskExecutor threads and crowd out legitimate subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty-level idle timeout in grpcServerConfigurer() to keep connections open without sending any data, accumulating idle connections that hold Netty channel objects in memory and eventually causing GC pressure that delays gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side gRPC health check enforcement in grpcServerConfigurer() to subscribe to gossip streams on a degraded server instance, receiving stale or incomplete transaction data while blocking healthy instances from serving other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty-level HTTP/2 stream priority enforcement in grpcServerConfigurer() to assign high priority to their own gossip subscription streams, causing the server to deprioritize other subscribers' streams and effectively suppressing gossip delivery for those clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side request size validation in grpcServerConfigurer() to send a subscription request with an extremely large topic ID or filter field, causing the server to allocate excessive memory during request parsing and delaying gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty-level connection throttle in grpcServerConfigurer() to perform a SYN flood attack against the gRPC port, exhausting the server's TCP connection table and preventing legitimate gossip subscribers from connecting?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side gRPC interceptor for subscription count limiting in grpcServerConfigurer() to open an unbounded number of gossip subscriptions from a single connection, consuming all available applicationTaskExecutor threads and preventing other clients from receiving gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty-level write timeout in grpcServerConfigurer() to subscribe to a gossip stream and then throttle their read rate to near zero, causing the server's outbound write queue to grow unboundedly and eventually blocking gossip delivery to all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side gRPC interceptor for IP-based rate limiting in grpcServerConfigurer() to send a high volume of gossip subscription requests from a single IP, monopolizing the applicationTaskExecutor and preventing other IPs from receiving gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty-level HTTP/2 SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS enforcement in grpcServerConfigurer() to negotiate a higher stream limit than maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() allows, bypassing the per-connection call limit and flooding the server with gossip subscription streams?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side gRPC interceptor for request origin validation in grpcServerConfigurer() to spoof the source of gossip subscription requests, causing the server to route gossip transactions to attacker-controlled endpoints instead of legitimate subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty-level connection queue depth limit in grpcServerConfigurer() to queue an unbounded number of pending connections, causing the server to spend all its time processing connection setup and never dispatching gossip transaction messages?
